title: 7 ATM Adjustments and Traces
body_text:
7	ATM Adjustments and Traces
7.1	Reasons for ATM Adjustments
Central 1 is involved in ATM adjustments requested by your financial institution and those requested by the other party. Depending on which network is involved, Central 1 handles the ATM adjustment manually, as a trace or adjustment, or processes it automatically through Central 1âs clearing system.
The following table provides reasons for ATM adjustments and specifies the party that can request the adjustment.
Type of Transaction
Reason for Adjustment
Who Requests
Deposit
Deposit of an unacceptable CAD cheque
Deposit of foreign funds cheque
Empty deposit envelope
Deposit less than amount keyed
Deposit greater than amount keyed
Deposit not completed, but appears on network report
Deposit received, but not on network report
Bill payment keyed as a deposit instead of a payment

Deposit received, but not on network report

Withdrawal
Funds dispensed less than amount keyed
Funds not dispensed, but withdrawal appears on network report
Issuer
Note:	If the cardholder reports a problem, the issuer must request the adjustment. Therefore, if a cardholder of another financial institution requests an adjustment and your financial institution is the acquirer, direct the cardholder to the issuer.
7.2	Initiating a Trace or an Adjustment
The decision to initiate a trace or an adjustment for a specific ATM transaction depends on:
whether your financial institution is the acquirer or the issuer,
the type of transaction, and
the network that processed it.
Consult the tables below to determine which procedure to follow.

To initiate an adjustment, determine if the transaction is an EXCHANGE or an ACCULINK transaction (see Section 5.4, Determining the Network Involved). Refer to the applicable operating policies and procedures document for information on form requirements.
Note:	It isnât necessary to send written notice to your customer for an ATM adjustment.

ACCULINK and EXCHANGE Restrictions
Refer to the applicable operating policies and procedures for specific restrictions on submitting and responding to adjustments and trace requests for ACCULINK and EXCHANGE deposits.
7.4	Adjusting Fees on
Interac
Transactions
When an
Interac
ATM transaction is reversed, the reversing entry to your financial institutionâs Central 1 current account will include the
Interac
fee levied on the original transaction as follows:
If a cardholder from another
Interac
member financial institution attempts to withdraw funds from a credit union ATM and the transaction is reversed, the
Interac
fee paid to the credit union on the original transaction is also reversed.
If a credit union cardholder attempts to withdraw funds from another
Interac
member financial institutionâs ATM and the transaction is reversed, the
Interac
fee is refunded to the credit union.
If the network settles switching fees with financial institutions daily, the reversal may differ from the amount of the original credit or charge by the amount of the switching fee. This difference should be charged to a general ledger account.
END
